name: Systems Security Engineering — Considerations for a Multidisciplinary Approach
owner: National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST)
description: NIST SP 800-160 Volume 1 (rev 1, 2022) provides foundational guidance on systems security engineering — the application of engineering and security principles throughout the system lifecycle. Cross-references ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288 system lifecycle processes.
relationToCloudSecurity: Architectural guidance applicable to cloud-system design.
relationToAISecurity: Engineering discipline applies to AI systems as to other engineered systems.

license.notes: Work of NIST is in the US public domain (17 USC §105).

current_extraction.notes: Source PDF marker-extracted to markdown. extract_800_160_v1.py parses the Appendix H security-engineering activities and tasks (aligned with ISO/IEC/IEEE 15288 system life cycle processes) into structured CSV/JSON: 108 activities and 474 tasks across 30 process prefixes (AA Acquisition, BA Business/Mission Analysis, AR Architecture Definition, RM Risk Management, SR Stakeholder Requirements, etc.). Each task carries its parent activity's title.

notes: Metadata stub; acquire from NIST CSRC and extract.
